messages.text: The court of Venice filled with senators, merchants, and citizens who wished the law would save Antonio without breaking itself.

messages.name: Duke
messages.text: Shylock, everyone expects a gentle answer. Take money, show mercy, and let the penalty sleep.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: I have sworn an oath. I want the penalty written in the bond. Ask me no reasons beyond my will.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: Do not argue with a stone tide. I am ready.
Bassanio offered twice the debt, then ten times, but Shylock refused each sum. Justice, he said, was his purchase.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: I beg the court to bend the law once and save a good man.
A young doctor of law entered with a letter from Bellario. It was Portia in disguise, carrying a calm face and a prepared mind.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Which is the merchant here, and which the Jew?
She read the bond and admitted that Venetian law gave Shylock a claim. Bassanio stared at the young judge without recognizing his wife.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then Shylock must be merciful.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: On what compulsion must I?

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The quality of mercy is not strained. It falls like a gentle drop from heaven and can bless both giver and receiver.
Her speech changed the air, but not Shylock. He praised her wisdom only when she confirmed the strict letter of the contract.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: A Daniel come to judgment. Wise young judge, give me my penalty.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Antonio, prepare your breast. The court awards the pound of flesh.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Take my life instead, my wife, all I have. Only save him.
Portia heard the wild offer and stored it away. Love spoke loudly, but the ring on Bassanio's finger remained to be tested.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Shylock, have a surgeon near, at your charge, to stop the blood.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: Is it so nominated in the bond?

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: It is not. Then proceed. But pause one moment. This bond gives no drop of blood.
Shylock sharpened his knife in public view. The sound made Bassanio flinch, but Antonio seemed almost peaceful, as if resistance were already spent.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: Give him his penalty. I am a tainted wether of the flock, meetest for death.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The court must honor law, or Venice loses the trust on which its commerce stands.
That sentence frightened Bassanio because it sounded like defeat. Portia let the fear grow until Shylock trusted her strict judgment completely.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: Most learned judge, a sentence! Come, prepare.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The bond is clear about flesh. It is silent about blood. Let justice keep to its own quality.
Only then did the room understand that exact words could protect life as fiercely as they had threatened it.
The drop of blood became the hinge of the trial, small enough to miss and strong enough to turn the whole court.
The Duke wanted mercy without knowing how to command it. Bassanio wanted justice bent without admitting that such bending could endanger every contract.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: A court cannot live by pity alone. If mercy comes, it must enter without destroying law.
That was Portia's genius. She did not defeat Shylock by ignoring strict words; she defeated him by reading them more strictly than he did.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: I stand for judgment. Let no one soften what I have purchased.
The knife, the scales, and the silent crowd turned abstract justice into something physical, immediate, and terrifying.
Every person in the court wanted an outcome, but Portia needed a method. Without method, mercy would look like favoritism and justice like murder.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Stand ready, Antonio. Stand ready, Shylock. The words you signed are about to speak for themselves.
The whole room leaned toward the bond.
